summary:
Methylation and acetylation modify genes' expressions via chemical marks on DNA and associated proteins; this regulation does not alter the base sequences directly. These processes play crucial roles in disease management due to their reversible nature and complex interplay influencing transcriptional outcomes dynamically. To summarize concisely yet comprehensively based solely upon critical aspects related to your query about non-sequence-altering effects leading towards altered gene activity patterns—Option A aligns perfectly given its focus on epigenetic modification techniques such as those involving cytosine-methylation & histones-acetylations which do exactly what was asked here i.e., influence RNA polymerase access thereby controlling protein synthesis whilst preserving genomic integrity intact throughout all stages including mitosis thus ensuring no lasting mutations occur post-treatment cessation if applicable per case scenario specifics involved therein too! #agentThought

甲基化和乙酰化通过修饰DNA和组蛋白来调节基因表达，而不直接改变碱基数序列。这些过程在疾病管理中具有重要性，因为它们是可逆的，并且能够动态地影响转录结果。#agentThought
